diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute panic/anxiety response over benign neutropenia
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Adrenergic symptoms are typical of a panic/sympathetic response.

symptoms.symptom_name: No measured fever / no true rigors

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No documented fever markedly lowers the probability of neutropenic sepsis; a measured fever would reverse this.

symptoms.symptom_name: No infective focus, bleeding or bruising

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No bleeding/bruising/organomegaly argues against acute leukaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symptom onset locked to reading the abnormal result; prior panic history

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute panic/anxiety response over benign neutropenia
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear anxiety trigger with prior panic history strongly supports a panic aetiology.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single most important immediate step — distinguishes benign anxiety from neutropenic fever.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Repeat FBC with differential and haematology review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirm the count is stable and consistent with benign neutropenia; not urgent.
